topologies.name: Cascade (Hegelian Dialectic)
topologies.description: Dialectical sequence: each agent attacks or refines the previous one's output. Thesis -> Antithesis -> iterated Synthesis.

workflow.description: Agent 1 produces output -> Agent 2 receives it and attacks/refines -> Agent 3 synthesizes

topologies.name: Star / Delphi
topologies.description: Moderator + N independent agents. Independent Round 1 (no cross-talk), then synthesis. Cognitive bagging.

workflow.description: Round 1: N agents respond independently. Round 2: agents see each other's output and revise their positions. The moderator synthesizes.

topologies.name: Adversarial + Jury
topologies.description: 2 agents debate, 3 jurors evaluate independently. Separates argumentation from judgment.

workflow.description: 2 advocates debate for N rounds, then 3 jurors with different constraints evaluate independently.

topologies.name: Ring (Iterated Bisociation)
topologies.description: Circular chain: each agent transforms the output into its own frame, and the last one passes it back to the first.

workflow.description: Agent 1 -> Agent 2 -> ... -> Agent N -> Agent 1. Each handoff forces a re-interpretation within the agent's frame.

topologies.name: Parallel Hats (De Bono)
topologies.description: N agents, each constrained to a different cognitive mode, operating in parallel.
topologies.theory: Six Thinking Hats (De Bono), Cognitive Diversity (Ashby)

workflow.description: Each agent operates in an exclusive cognitive mode. Output is aggregated without contamination.

topologies.name: Steelman Chain
topologies.description: 3 rounds of progressive strengthening, then 1-2 adversarial rounds against the strongest version.
topologies.theory: Steelmanning, Principle of Charity + Adversarial Collaboration

workflow.description: Phase 1: N agents strengthen the argument in sequence. Phase 2: 1-2 agents attack the strongest version.

topologies.name: Socratic Drill
topologies.description: A chain of questions that digs down to the fundamental assumptions.
topologies.theory: Metodo Socratico / Elenchus, Key Assumptions Check (CIA)

workflow.description: The interrogator agent asks 'why?'. The responding agent defends. The interrogator digs down to the undeclared assumptions.

topologies.name: Scenario Matrix
topologies.description: 2 critical uncertainties generate 4 quadrants. 4 agents each explore one scenario.

workflow.description: 2 axes of uncertainty -> 4 scenarios. Each agent explores one scenario, assuming it as reality. Then comparison.

topologies.name: Bisociation Engine
topologies.description: Forced collision between incompatible domains to generate novel connections.

workflow.description: Each agent analyzes the topic from its forced domain. A synthesizer finds the structural connections.

topologies.name: Wise Mind
topologies.description: Emotional Mind + Rational Mind -> Wise Mind synthesis. Not dialectic but integration.
topologies.theory: DBT Wise Mind (Linehan, 1993), Dual Process Theory

workflow.description: The emotional agent produces an emotional perspective. The rational agent produces a rational perspective. The wise agent integrates.

topologies.name: SCAMPER Parallel
topologies.description: 7 agents, each constrained to a different SCAMPER operation.

workflow.description: 7 agents apply S-C-A-M-P-E-R in parallel. The moderator synthesizes the best ideas.

topologies.name: Assumption Inversion
topologies.description: Identifies assumptions, inverts them systematically, and explores the implications.

workflow.description: Phase 1: Socratic Drill to list the assumptions. Phase 2: classification by importance/certainty. Phase 3: inversion of the top-K assumptions.

topologies.name: Sequential Deep Chain
topologies.description: Constraints chained in sequence on the same cognitive thread. Each round receives the output of all previous ones and integrates it with a new constraint.
topologies.theory: Chain-of-Thought (Wei et al. 2022), Self-Consistency (Wang et al. 2023), Deliberative Alignment

workflow.description: Each round applies a different constraint to the same problem. The agent integrates the output of the previous rounds as context.

steps.description: First analysis with the primary constraint
Integration of the tensions between rounds 1 and 2
Final calibration: what remains after the stress-test?
